commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0f5aee0d16055f6c9392d7df13aa24ef3e3ce67d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an Anthropic-local `apiKeyHelper` settings probe and synthetic auth marker so `claude-cli` can pass the pre-spawn auth gate without stored Claude CLI credentials.

PR surface: Source +68, Tests +36. Total +104 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main returns `undefined` synthetic auth when the shared Claude CLI credential reader finds no Keychain or `.credentials.json` credential, so the auth gate can throw `missing-provider-auth`; I did not run a live proxy-backed OpenClaw turn.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor needs to provide real helper-auth proof and choose/consolidate the canonical implementation before merge.

target.title: fix(claude-cli): treat apiKeyHelper in settings.json as valid auth
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When Claude CLI is authenticated via `apiKeyHelper` in `~/.claude/settings.json` (the documented mechanism for corporate gateways and dynamic/proxy auth), OpenClaw's pre-spawn auth-gate returns `missing-provider-auth` (`No API key found for provider "anthropic"`), even though the Claude CLI itself authenticates and runs correctly.

The root cause: `readClaudeCliCredentials` / `readClaudeCliCredentialsCached` only reads macOS keychain or `~/.claude/.credentials.json`. There is no path for `apiKeyHelper`. When neither source has credentials, `resolveClaudeCliSyntheticAuth()` returns `undefined` — and both the provider-discovery descriptor and the runtime registration short-circuit before Claude is ever spawned.

This patch adds `hasClaudeCliApiKeyHelper()` to `cli-auth-seam.ts` (same owner boundary, same file-FS injection pattern as `extensions/google/oauth.settings.ts`). Both `resolveClaudeCliSyntheticAuth` functions now fall through to check `apiKeyHelper` when no stored credential exists. If present, they return a sentinel auth result (`CLAUDE_CLI_API_KEY_HELPER_MARKER`) that passes the gate — the actual key is still fetched by the Claude CLI helper script at spawn time, so no real secret ever touches OpenClaw.

Fixes #97489.

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed:** Linux users (or any host without `~/.claude/.credentials.json`) who use `apiKeyHelper` for proxy auth get `missing-provider-auth` before Claude CLI is spawned, blocking all claude-cli model routes.

**Real environment tested:** macOS local source checkout, Node 22.22.0, upstream/main SHA `08d15ec32d`.

**Exact commands run after this patch:**

```
pnpm test extensions/anthropic/index.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
pnpm test extensions/anthropic -- --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix — full anthropic extension suite:**

```
Test Files  7 passed (7)
     Tests  105 passed (105)
  Duration  8.74s
```

New tests covering the exact bug path:

```
✓ resolves claude-cli synthetic auth for apiKeyHelper when no stored credential   1ms
✓ returns undefined synthetic auth when no credential and no apiKeyHelper         0ms
```

Both paths through `resolveSyntheticAuth` (`provider-discovery.ts` and `register.runtime.ts`) are covered by the same mock, since both consume `claudeCliAuth.*` from `cli-auth-seam.ts`.

**Observed result after fix:** `resolveSyntheticAuth({ provider: "claude-cli" })` returns `{ apiKey: "claude-cli-api-key-helper", source: "Claude CLI apiKeyHelper", mode: "api-key" }` when no stored credential exists but `apiKeyHelper` is declared. The existing OAuth/token paths are unchanged.

**What was not tested:** Live end-to-end proof on a Linux host with an actual `apiKeyHelper` script + proxy — that requires a Crabbox Linux lane with a mock proxy. The source-level and unit proof fully covers the auth-gate gap described in the issue. E2E proof can follow in a live Docker lane if maintainers request it.
target.author: hugenshen
